profile.profile_text:
ALEXANDER J. NACHMAN
Port Washington, NY | (516) 998-8530 | ajn82@cornell.edu |  LinkedIn

Rising junior at Cornell University studying Industrial and Labor Relations with a concentration in Economics. Interested in how data, strategy, and storytelling connect to shape the business and media side of sports. Combines analytical thinking with clear communication and collaborative experience from marketing and outreach internships.
EDUCATION
Cornell University | School of Industrial and Labor Relations 	Ithaca, NY
Bachelor of Science in Industrial and Labor Relations – Concentration: Economics  GPA: 3.46		    Class of 2028
Coursework: Macroeconomics, Statistics & Data Science, Calculus, Introduction to Personality, Moral Dilemmas in the Law
Schreiber High School	Port Washington, NY
Weighted GPA: 4.68      ACT: 36		    Class of 2024
Awards: National Merit Scholar Finalist, AP Scholar with Distinction, National Honor Society, Foreign Language Honor Society, Mu Alpha Theta Mathematics Honor Society 
AP Coursework: Statistics, Psychology, Calculus, Biology, Physics, U.S. History, European History, French 	
INTERNSHIP EXPERIENCE
Marketing Intern | City and State Magazine – New York, NY	            Jun 2025 – Current
Partner with the VP of Digital Growth to manage and analyze prospect data, supporting outreach strategy and business development targeting nonprofit segments.
Maintain and enhance multi-year datasets for campaign targeting and audience segmentation, optimizing sales team outreach for sponsorship and award programs.
 Research Intern | City and State Magazine – New York, NY					     Jun 2023 – Aug 2023
Researched and compiled contact lists of nonprofit and public-sector organizations to support outreach, marketing, and editorial planning.
Organized and maintained internal records in Google Sheets to streamline access for sales and development teams.
Intern | NYS Congressional Voter Outreach – Great Neck, NY     			                   Sep 2022 – Nov 2022
Continued voter engagement efforts after the primary election, focusing on increasing voter turnout across key districts.
Supported regional outreach operations through phone banking, event staffing, and logistical support.
Campaign Intern | Jon Kaiman for Congress – Great Neck, NY 				     Jun 2022 – Aug 2022 
Conducted voter outreach through door-to-door canvassing and phone banking in support of campaign’s primary efforts.
Assisted in organizing fundraising events and community meet-and-greets to increase candidate visibility and engagement.
SELECTED ACADEMIC EXPERIENCE
Applied economic modeling and data analysis frameworks to evaluate market behavior and global trends.
Used R and RStudio in Statistics and Data Science coursework to perform regression analysis and interpret real-world datasets.
Strengthened analytical writing and critical reasoning through coursework in Moral Dilemmas in the Law and Personality Psychology.
ADDITIONAL EXPERIENCE
Golf Attendant | Village Club of Sands Point – Port Washington, NY  	Jun 2023 – Aug 2023
Helped maintain the driving range and organized golf equipment to support day-to-day club operations.
Golf Caddy | North Hempstead Country Club – Port Washington, NY  	   Jun 2021 – Aug 2021	
Assisted golfers during rounds by carrying clubs, providing yardage estimates, and helping locate golf balls.
LEADERSHIP & INVOLVEMENT
Cornell Sports Business Society | Member - Engages with professionals/case studies on sports media and event management.
Cornell University Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity | Member 
Sigma Nu Fraternity  | Social Chairman

jobText:
About the job
Marketing AI Strategy – Summer Internship 

About Gen: 

Gen is a global company dedicated to powering Digital Freedom through its trusted consumer brands including Norton, Avast, LifeLock, MoneyLion and more. Our combined heritage is rooted in financial empowerment and cyber safety for the first digital generations, and today we deliver award-winning cybersecurity, online privacy, identity protection and financial wellness solutions to nearly 500 million users in more than 150 countries.

Together, we share a collective passion and vision to protect consumers and help them grow, manage, and secure their digital and financial lives. We’re always looking for smart, fearless and high-impact talent who see AI as a teammate – leveraging it to move faster and deliver meaningful results.

When you’re part of Gen, you’ll have the flexibility, tools and support to do your best work and grow your career – from flexible working options and time off to competitive pay, benefits and well-being programs.

At Gen, we are scrappy and relentlessly customer driven. We create room for healthy debate, experimentation, and continuous learning, and we seek out people with different experiences, identities and ideas to join our team. You’ll work with people who back each other, respect each other and understand that our differences are a competitive advantage.

If this sounds like you, we’d love you to be part of Gen.

About The Role

What are you doing this summer? If you’re looking for an internship that gives you hands-on experience in a fast-paced, high-impact technology environment—especially one at the forefront of cybersecurity—you’re in the right place.

This is an opportunity to grow your skills, build your resume, and make a real impact.

Key Responsibilities

Audit and analyze outputs from the Hightouch AI decisioning engine to identify gaps in personalized content across products, segments, and lifecycle stages 
Develop new modular email and push notification content aligned to AI-selected offers and user insights 
Build reusable content frameworks designed specifically for dynamic, AI-driven personalization 
Support the strategy and documentation of new AI “agents,” including defining use cases, inputs, outputs, and success metrics 
Partner with Martech and BI teams to evaluate performance of AI-driven journeys and recommend optimizations 
Help design structured experimentation plans to test AI-generated content vs. traditional lifecycle messaging 
Contribute to internal documentation and best practices for scaling AI-enabled marketing workflows 


About You

You are currently enrolled in a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree program.

You’re curious about how AI is used in marketing, and you’re eager to learn how teams personalize content at scale.

Personal Attributes: 

Visionary leader with a strategic mindset and a passion for leveraging data to drive decision making. 
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, high-tech environment and manage complex problem 


Internship Details 

Start Date: June 1

Duration: 10 weeks

NYC Office 5 days per week

Pay Rate: $30.00
